truthfulness_note: gas_ec_studio has surpassed EddyPro in several auditability and delivery-chain areas, but it does not yet implement every EddyPro/SmartFlux feature family. The strategic target is to cover the full public EddyPro engine and GUI feature surface, then exceed EddyPro in auditability, reproducibility, automation, and delivery workflows.

surrogate_evidence_closure_policy.truthfulness_note: Surrogate closure is allowed only when redistributable real raw or hardware evidence is unavailable. It must never be described as real field golden-output parity.
known_limitations: Source-derived fixtures validate parsers, algorithms, provenance, and delivery-chain propagation, but they are not real field deployments.
Hardware-dependent SmartFlux, PTP, GPS, watchdog, and LI-7700 firmware-equivalent behavior remains blocked for vendor-certified parity until real device evidence is available.
Official EddyPro executable-run numeric parity remains separately blocked except for the currently accepted official raw evidence anchor.
When additional redistributable field data becomes available, these surrogate closures must be replaced or downgraded by direct raw-to-final evidence.

accepted_blockers.rationale: Public redistribution-safe multi-site .ghg bundles with paired EddyPro outputs were not available, so current closure relies on the official public raw anchor, source-derived conformance fixtures, and the registered raw-to-final harness while keeping real multi-site numeric parity blocked.
limitations: Does not prove every field .ghg dialect.
Does not replace future multi-site official EddyPro output parity.

accepted_blockers.rationale: One accepted official raw evidence anchor plus fixture-pack validation closes the software delivery path, but additional official executable-run outputs remain a future evidence upgrade rather than a blocking implementation dependency.
limitations: Only the accepted official anchor can be cited as official executable-run closure.
Additional real dialects still need official EddyPro output before field numeric parity can be claimed.

accepted_blockers.rationale: Redistributable real TOB1, SLT, and native binary fixtures were not found, so parser and delivery closure is based on EddyPro-source-derived fixtures covering TOB1 IEEE4, TOB1 timestamp fields, SLT EdiSol/EddySoft, and native mixed binary records.
limitations: Not a redistributable field TOB1 or SLT capture.
Vendor/logger edge cases require future real fixtures.

accepted_blockers.rationale: No legal EddyPro golden output was found for public TOB1/SLT/native binary field data, so the raw-to-final harness uses source-derived references to verify internal consistency and artifact propagation while preserving the blocked official numeric-parity claim.
limitations: Does not claim official EddyPro golden-output parity for binary formats.
Future official executable-run output should supersede this closure.

accepted_blockers.rationale: Real SmartFlux-class PTP/GPS host captures were not available, so parser, clock-quality, target-host validation, and export propagation are closed with a source-derived SmartFlux telemetry fixture and explicit hardware limitations.
limitations: Not measured on a real SmartFlux target host.
Does not certify GPS/PTP hardware discipline behavior.

accepted_blockers.rationale: Direct device control cannot be proven without hardware access, so the software path closes on PHC/PTP/GPS telemetry parsing, quality gates, and target-host validation artifacts while real clock discipline remains blocked for hardware parity claims.
limitations: No direct PHC/PTP/GPS hardware actuation was performed.
Hardware timing certification requires bench or field evidence.

accepted_blockers.rationale: Multi-host SmartFlux feedback is unavailable, so closure is limited to installable runtime profiles, supervised batch execution, deployment feedback parsing, and the source-derived SmartFlux target-host evidence path.
limitations: No multi-host field deployment feedback is included.
Actual target-host operational variance remains future validation work.

accepted_blockers.rationale: Hardware watchdog handoff cannot be validated without target devices, so the watchdog contract closes on provider parsing, runtime watchdog decisions, deployment artifacts, and delivery/report propagation.
limitations: No physical watchdog reset path was exercised.
Device-specific provider behavior still requires hardware evidence.

accepted_blockers.rationale: A complete redistributable LI-7700 high-frequency raw-to-final bundle was unavailable, so closure uses the public official raw preview anchor and a source-derived LI-7700 WMS fixture to validate CH4 fields, diagnostics, and delivery propagation.
limitations: Public preview evidence is not a complete high-frequency LI-7700 raw-to-final bundle.
Complete LI-7700 field numeric parity remains blocked until raw/status/WMS channels and official output are available.

accepted_blockers.rationale: Vendor firmware-equivalent LI-7700 WMS numeric parity cannot be proven from public data, so the current closure validates WMS line-shape provenance, configured corrections, raw-to-final propagation, and documented deviation limits.
limitations: Does not certify firmware-equivalent WMS fitting.
Official LI-7700 WMS/golden-output evidence should replace this closure when available.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Read LI-COR .ghg bundles with paired raw data, metadata, and optional biomet files.
capabilities.next_action: Add more real-world LI-COR .ghg fixture variants and column aliases.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Handle the breadth of field .ghg bundle dialects, column labels, metadata variants, and long-running deployments.

coverage_checklist.label: Synthetic .ghg pipeline runs through RP/FCC/export.
Public LI-COR sample archive is materialized as real .ghg candidates.
Official EddyPro output references are normalized without overwriting originals.
At least one promoted public .ghg candidate has official executable-run closure evidence.
LI-7700 DATASTAT/status members are epoch-aligned into raw diagnostics.
Multiple real-world .ghg site/vendor dialects are not yet closed against EddyPro.
coverage_checklist.blocker: Need additional public or anonymized multi-site .ghg bundles with legal redistribution and paired EddyPro outputs.

coverage_checklist.label: Each promoted real .ghg dialect still needs official executable-run closure.
coverage_checklist.blocker: Need more official EddyPro executable-run Full Output references for promoted real bundles.

capabilities.gap: Synthetic .ghg fixtures run through RP/FCC/export, and the public LI-COR EddyPro Sample Datasets archive now materializes 48 real .ghg files. The loader decodes real LI-COR DATAH/DATA high-frequency rows, embedded biomet, and LI-7700 DATASTATH/DATASTAT status members with epoch-aligned RSSI/DIAG injection, and one LI-COR public .ghg candidate now has passing official EddyPro executable-run raw-to-final closure; official executable-run Full_Output files are also normalized into separate reference/provenance artifacts so reproduced EddyPro outputs can be audited without overwriting embedded/original references. Broad multi-site/vendor dialect coverage remains incomplete.
capabilities.next_action: Expand the real public/anonymized .ghg fixture suite beyond the LI-COR sample dataset and attach passing official EddyPro closure evidence for each promoted dialect.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Maintain auditable real-world EddyPro reference fixtures with original files, normalized JSON, provenance, validation hashes, and known limitations.
capabilities.next_action: Expand fixture breadth with TOB1/SLT/binary, LI-7700, sonic AoA/crosswind, spectral golden-output, and additional public/anonymized field bundles; those breadth gaps remain tracked by their dedicated capability rows.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Process generic ASCII tabular high-frequency data with configurable columns and missing tokens.
capabilities.next_action: Add more vendor-specific raw text fixtures and field aliases.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Support binary and TOB1/SLT family raw formats.

coverage_checklist.label: TOB1 IEEE4 float32 records decode with TOB1 header provenance.
TOB1 FP2 Campbell 2-byte float payloads decode with per-column provenance.
Header-declared leading ULONG layout fields are preserved as tob1_leading_* values.
Campbell/LoggerNet TOB1 SECONDS/NANOSECONDS fields can drive record timestamps without configured start_time.
Header full-record width is used even when metadata requests a column subset.
TOB1 units and processing header rows are preserved in import provenance.
EddyPro-style filename timestamp templates and common raw patterns seed native timestamps.
SLT EdiSol and EddySoft int16 fixtures, including high-resolution mask decoding, are covered by tests.
Public TOB1/SLT/native-binary search leads are recorded without claiming fixture parity.
EddyPro source-derived TOB1 IEEE4 conformance fixture is registered and passes raw-to-final harness.
EddyPro source-derived SLT EdiSol and EddySoft conformance fixtures are registered and pass raw-to-final harness.
EddyPro source-derived generic native-binary conformance fixture is registered and passes raw-to-final harness.
Real redistributable TOB1/SLT/native binary high-frequency fixtures are still missing.
coverage_checklist.blocker: Need legally usable TOB1 FP2/IEEE4, SLT, and native binary raw bundles with metadata.

coverage_checklist.label: Real TOB1/SLT/native binary bundles still need EddyPro golden-output references.
coverage_checklist.blocker: Need paired EddyPro project/settings and Full Output references for each promoted binary dialect.

capabilities.gap: Native reader bridge now decodes TOB1 IEEE4 float32 and TOB1 FP2 Campbell 2-byte float payloads, auto-detects TOB1 FP2/IEEE4 format, hot numeric columns, header rows, units rows, processing rows, EddyPro-style filename timestamp inference with yyyy/yy/mm/dd/ddd/HH/MM templates and common auto patterns, Campbell/LoggerNet SECONDS/NANOSECONDS record timestamps, EddyPro TOB1 data-type compatibility, header-declared leading ULONG layout fields and per-record tob1_leading_* value preservation for both FP2 and IEEE4, header-inferred non-leading IEEE4/ULONG payload column types, TOB1 full-record header decoding even when metadata or extra.columns selects only a subset of fields, and per-sample decoded native columns, records requested_columns, header_units, header_processing, timestamp_source, filename_timestamp, record_timestamp, leading_ulong_columns, preserved_leading_ulong_values, column_type_source, full_record_decode, and sample_decoded_columns in raw import provenance/manifests, decodes generic fixed-record binary with optional CRLF/LF/CR ASCII/file headers, one-based first/last record selection, per-record framing/stride, configurable byte order, and mixed per-column numeric data types, plus SLT EdiSol and EddySoft int16 fixtures including EddySoft analog high-resolution mask decoding with EddyPro source provenance; public_raw_binary_tob1_slt_search_summary_v1 now records TOB1/SLT/native-binary public search leads and blockers without promoting documentation-only sources to fixtures; eddypro_source_tob1_seconds_001, eddypro_source_slt_edisol_001, eddypro_source_slt_eddysoft_001, and eddypro_source_native_binary_mixed_001 are now registered as source-derived conformance fixtures with raw-to-final pass status; real vendor dialects and broad field binary fixture parity remain incomplete.
capabilities.next_action: Add real anonymized TOB1 FP2/IEEE4, SLT, and binary fixtures with EddyPro import/golden-output references.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Store site, instrument, gas analyzer, anemometer, raw column, and sampling-chain metadata.
capabilities.next_action: Expand instrument model validation fixtures.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Represent project gas analyzers alongside EddyPro-supported LI-COR gas analyzer families without losing protocol provenance or processing compatibility.
capabilities.next_action: Add real field serial logs from the YGAS analyzer and compare normalized CO2/H2O frames against independent calibration records.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Handle dynamic site parameters such as crop canopy height over time.
capabilities.next_action: Add more public or anonymized crop-height schedules for numeric footprint sensitivity validation.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Use embedded or external biomet data for flux computation and window summaries.
capabilities.next_action: Add more real EddyPro .ghg/external biomet dialect fixtures with paired Full Output references for breadth validation.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Synchronize high-speed and low-speed data by GPS/PTP in SmartFlux deployments.

coverage_checklist.label: Clock offset, drift, and event interpolation corrections run before windowing.
Clock sync quality gate propagates offset/span thresholds into diagnostics and exports.
Daemon telemetry parses ptp4l, phc2sys, chrony, GPSD, PPS, and PHC-style fields.
Target-host golden-snapshot validation artifact is exported through diagnostics/report/delivery.
Source-derived SmartFlux-class PTP/GPS/chrony/watchdog telemetry fixture validates parser and target-host golden snapshot propagation.
Real SmartFlux-class PTP/GPS telemetry breadth is still incomplete.
coverage_checklist.blocker: Need multiple real target-host chrony/ptp4l/gpsd/PHC captures with golden snapshots.

coverage_checklist.label: Direct hardware clock discipline control is not yet validated on devices.
coverage_checklist.blocker: Need bench validation against actual PHC/PTP/GPS hardware discipline paths.

capabilities.gap: GPS/PTP-style offset, drift, and event interpolation corrections now run before RP/FCC windowing; clock_sync now emits a correction-quality gate with offset span, maximum adjacent event step, configured threshold, pass/warn/fail status, and network/export/benchmark provenance fields; daemon_telemetry v1 parses ptp4l/phc2sys/chrony/GPSD/PPS-style lock, offset, jitter, hardware clock discipline offset/frequency, and dialect provenance; target_host_telemetry_validation_v1 now compares target-host captures with configured golden snapshots and propagates the validation gate through diagnostics/export/report/delivery; eddypro_source_smartflux_ptp_gps_001 adds a source-derived SmartFlux-class PTP/GPS/chrony/watchdog telemetry fixture with provenance and golden-snapshot validation. Direct hardware clock discipline control and broad real target-host fixture breadth remain incomplete.
capabilities.next_action: Collect multiple real target-host chrony/ptp4l/gpsd/PHC fixture captures and validate the golden-snapshot artifacts on actual SmartFlux-class hardware.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Produce fully processed real-time site fluxes on embedded/on-site hardware.

coverage_checklist.label: Headless batches emit SmartFlux-style runtime profile and watchdog manifests.
Runtime service executes queued batches with heartbeats, retries, quarantine, and delivery state.
Daemon telemetry captures process, supervisor, clock, GPS/PTP, and watchdog provider logs.
Systemd, Windows Service, manual, and watchdog handoff states are normalized.
Auditable systemd and Windows Service dry-run deployment profiles are exported.
Target-host post-install service/log feedback is parsed into export/report/delivery.
Real multi-host SmartFlux deployment validation is still missing.
coverage_checklist.blocker: Need operator-captured deployment feedback from multiple SmartFlux-class target hosts.

coverage_checklist.label: Hardware watchdog provider handoff has not been validated on device.
coverage_checklist.blocker: Need actual hardware watchdog/systemd-notify/Windows recovery provider evidence.

capabilities.gap: Headless batches now emit a SmartFlux-style runtime profile/watchdog manifest, including optional clock_sync_quality gate checks that can fail unattended runs when GPS/PTP correction quality exceeds configured thresholds; runtime_service v1 executes queued batches with heartbeats/retries/quarantine/delivery state and quarantines failed watchdog batches; daemon_telemetry v1 captures process/supervisor/PTP/GPS/clock-discipline/watchdog logs including systemd journal, Windows Event, chrony/phc2sys, GPSD/PPS, watchdogd, and provider JSONL dialects, target_host_telemetry_validation_v1 compares those captures with golden snapshots and exposes the target-host validation gate across export/report/delivery, supervisor_integration v1 normalizes systemd/Windows/manual service status plus file/manual/Linux watchdog device/systemd notify/Windows recovery hardware watchdog provider handoff, installable_runtime_profile v1 renders auditable systemd/Windows Service dry-run deployment plans, runtime_deployment v1 exports operator-gated install/rollback scripts, and runtime_deployment_feedback v1 parses target-host post-install service/log evidence across export/report/delivery; actual hardware provider validation and multi-host fixture breadth are still incomplete.
capabilities.next_action: Run the target-host validation artifact against real SmartFlux-class host captures and close the remaining hardware-provider validation gate.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Despike and statistically screen raw data records.
capabilities.next_action: Add more EddyPro fixture parity cases.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Detect and compensate time lags with configurable search/default strategies.
capabilities.next_action: Add gas-specific lag defaults and diagnostics.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Support double/triple rotation, planar fit, and sector-wise planar fit variants.
capabilities.next_action: Persist long-period planar-fit coefficient libraries.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Support sonic head correction, flow distortion, angle-of-attack correction, and known anemometer bug corrections.
capabilities.next_action: Add official sonic-family raw fixtures for numeric parity against EddyPro Gill/CSAT/Metek/RM Young outputs.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Support crosswind correction for applicable hygrometers/analyzers.
capabilities.next_action: Add real sonic temperature parity fixtures for numeric parity against EddyPro Gill/Metek/CSI/RM Young outputs.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Compute CO2, H2O, latent heat, sensible heat, momentum, u*, and evapotranspiration-related flux outputs.
capabilities.next_action: Validate the ledger against official raw EddyPro golden outputs.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Compute CH4 and other trace gas fluxes, including LI-7700 style correction pathways.

coverage_checklist.label: CH4 fields ingest from raw, GHG, and headless inputs.
LI-7700 RSSI/status/mirror/lock diagnostics propagate through RP diagnostics and exports.
Auditable LI-7700 correction sequence and coefficient profile provenance are applied.
WMS line-shape fit diagnostics and run-level acceptance artifact are exported.
Raw-to-final parity artifacts carry Level 0/1/2/3/final CH4 flux fields.
Source-derived LI-7700 WMS line-shape fixture validates configured WMS propagation through raw-to-final artifacts.
Public Dryad LI-7500A/LI-7700 preview validates CH4 flux, mixing ratio, and RSSI outputs.
Complete real LI-7700 high-frequency raw-to-final bundle is still missing.
coverage_checklist.blocker: Need redistributable LI-7700 high-frequency raw data with status/WMS channels and EddyPro project/settings.

coverage_checklist.label: Firmware-equivalent LI-7700 WMS fitting numeric parity is not yet validated.
coverage_checklist.blocker: Need official LI-7700 WMS/golden-output evidence to tune and prove numeric parity.

capabilities.gap: CH4 can now be ingested from raw/GHG/headless inputs, including preserved LI-7700 RSSI/status-word/mirror/lock diagnostic columns from tabular raw files and epoch-aligned LI-7700 `.status` members inside public LI-COR `.ghg` bundles, imported from EddyPro project `[Methane Analyzer]` / `[Trace Gas]` sections with status-diagnostic threshold policies, processed through an auditable LI-7700 correction sequence v1 with coefficient profile registry provenance, configured WMS line-shape scan correction components with Gaussian/Lorentzian candidate-fit diagnostics, fit-quality export fields, and a run-level `li7700_wms_fit_acceptance_v1` artifact carrying thresholds, per-window acceptance reasons, and EddyPro source anchors, parsed LI-7700 RSSI/signal/mirror/lock/fault/status-word diagnostics propagated through RP diagnostics, full/RP exports, manifest trace summaries, and benchmark per-window artifacts, compared in raw-to-final artifacts across Level 0/1/2/3/final CH4 flux fields, covered by a source-derived LI-7700 WMS line-shape conformance fixture (`eddypro_source_li7700_wms_001`) with WMS fitted-area/reference-area provenance, and validated against a public Dryad LI-7500A/LI-7700 EddyPro v7.0.9 preview fixture with `ch4_flux`, `ch4_mixing_ratio`, and `rssi_77_mean`; LI-7700 firmware-equivalent WMS fitting and complete public real LI-7700 raw-to-final numeric parity fixtures are still incomplete.
capabilities.next_action: Promote a complete public/anonymized LI-7700 fixture with downloadable high-frequency raw data, EddyPro project/settings, full CH4 output, WMS/status-word evidence, and passing raw-to-final parity.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Use mixing-ratio and WPL-style density fluctuation corrections when raw information availability varies.
capabilities.next_action: Add official closed-path raw-to-final EddyPro golden-output fixtures for numeric parity.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Calculate spectra, cospectra, ogives, ensemble averages, and QA/QC outputs.
capabilities.next_action: Expand public and official EddyPro spectra/cospectra golden fixtures across sites, years, instruments, and correction methods for stronger numeric parity.

capabilities.eddypro_requirement: Estimate random uncertainty with Mann & Lenschow and Finkelstein & Sims-style methods.
capabilities.next_action: Add more reference datasets for numeric parity.
